accident: An accident is something that happens by chance, often causing damage or a problem.
accidentally: If you do something accidentally, you do it by mistake and did not mean to.
actual: Actual means real or true, not guessed or imagined.
actually: Actually is used to say what is really true, especially when it is different from what someone expected.
address: An address is the details of where a person or place can be found, such as a house number and street.
answer: An answer is what you say or write in reply to a question.
appear: To appear means to come into sight or seem to be a certain way.
arrive: To arrive means to reach the place you were travelling to.
believe: To believe means to think that something is true or to trust someone.
bicycle: A bicycle is a vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pedalling.
breath: A breath is the air you take into or let out of your lungs.
breathe: To breathe means to take air into your lungs and let it out again.
build: To build means to make something by putting parts together.
busy: Busy means having a lot to do or full of activity.
business: A business is work or an organisation that sells goods or services.
calendar: A calendar shows days, weeks and months of the year.
caught: Caught is the past tense of catch; it can mean grabbed, trapped or noticed doing something.
centre: The centre is the middle point or main part of something.
century: A century is a period of one hundred years.
certain: Certain means sure, or known to be true.
circle: A circle is a round shape where every point on the edge is the same distance from the centre.
complete: Complete means finished or with nothing missing.
consider: To consider means to think carefully about something before deciding.
continue: To continue means to keep going and not stop.
decide: To decide means to choose after thinking about the options.
describe: To describe means to say or write what someone or something is like.
difficult: Difficult means hard to do, understand or deal with.
disappear: To disappear means to stop being seen or to go away.
early: Early means before the usual or expected time.
earth: Earth can mean the planet we live on, or soil and ground.
eight: Eight is the number after seven and before nine.
eighth: Eighth means number eight in order.
enough: Enough means as much or as many as needed.
exercise: Exercise is physical activity that keeps your body healthy, or a task for practice.
experience: Experience is something that happens to you, or knowledge you gain by doing something.
experiment: An experiment is a careful test to find out what happens or whether an idea is true.
extreme: Extreme means very great, strong or far from the usual.
famous: Famous means known by many people.
february: February is the second month of the year.
forward: Forward means towards the front or ahead.
forwards: Forwards means towards the front or ahead.
fruit: Fruit is the part of a plant that often has seeds and can be eaten, such as an apple.
grammar: Grammar is the set of rules for how words fit together in a language.
group: A group is a number of people or things together.
guard: To guard means to protect or watch over someone or something.
guide: A guide is someone or something that shows the way or gives helpful information.
heard: Heard is the past tense of hear; it means listened to or noticed a sound.
heart: The heart is the organ that pumps blood around the body; it can also mean feelings.
height: Height is how tall or high something is.
history: History is the study of things that happened in the past.
imagine: To imagine means to make a picture or idea in your mind.
increase: To increase means to become bigger in number, size or amount.
important: Important means having great value or needing attention.
interest: Interest is a feeling of wanting to know or learn more about something.
island: An island is land with water all around it.
knowledge: Knowledge is what you know and understand.
learn: To learn means to gain knowledge or a skill.
length: Length is how long something is from one end to the other.
library: A library is a place where books and information are kept for people to use.
material: Material is the substance something is made from, such as cotton, wood or metal.
medicine: Medicine is something used to help treat illness or pain.
mention: To mention means to say something briefly.
minute: A minute is a period of sixty seconds.
natural: Natural means found in nature or not made by people.
naughty: Naughty means behaving badly or not following rules.
notice: To notice means to see, hear or become aware of something.
occasion: An occasion is a particular time when something happens, often something special.
occasionally: Occasionally means sometimes, but not often.
often: Often means many times or frequently.
opposite: Opposite means completely different or facing the other way.
ordinary: Ordinary means normal, usual or not special.
particular: Particular means a specific one, not just any one.
popular: Popular means liked or enjoyed by many people.
position: Position is where something is, or the way it is placed.
possess: To possess something means to own it or have it.
possession: A possession is something that you own or have.
possible: Possible means able to happen or be done.
potatoes: Potatoes are round root vegetables that grow underground.
pressure: Pressure is force pushing on something, or stress caused by demands.
probably: Probably means very likely to happen or be true.
promise: A promise is a firm statement that you will do something.
purpose: Purpose is the reason something exists or is done.
quarter: A quarter is one of four equal parts.
question: A question is something you ask to find out information.
recent: Recent means having happened not long ago.
regular: Regular means happening again and again in the same pattern, or usual.
reign: A reign is the time when a king or queen rules.
remember: To remember means to keep something in your mind or bring it back to mind.
sentence: A sentence is a group of words that expresses a complete idea.
separate: Separate means apart, not joined together.
special: Special means different from the usual in an important or valued way.
straight: Straight means not curved, bent or crooked.
strength: Strength is the power to lift, push, resist or keep going.
suppose: To suppose means to think something is true, often without being completely sure.
therefore: Therefore means for that reason or as a result.
though: Though means although, or despite the fact that something is true.
although: Although means even though or despite the fact that.
thought: A thought is an idea in your mind; thought is also the past tense of think.
through: Through means moving in one side and out of the other, or from start to finish.
weight: Weight is how heavy something is.
woman: A woman is an adult female person.
women: Women is the plural of woman.
accommodate: To accommodate means to provide space for someone or something, or to fit in with needs.
accompany: To accompany means to go somewhere with someone or to happen at the same time.
according: According to something means as stated by it, or in a way that agrees with it.
achieve: To achieve means to succeed in doing something through effort.
aggressive: Aggressive means ready to attack, argue or behave forcefully.
amateur: An amateur is someone who does an activity for interest, not as a paid job.
ancient: Ancient means very old or from a long time ago.
apparent: Apparent means easy to see or seeming to be true.
appreciate: To appreciate means to understand the value of something or be thankful for it.
attached: Attached means joined, fastened or feeling emotionally close.
available: Available means ready to be used or free to do something.
average: Average means typical, ordinary, or the middle amount found by sharing equally.
awkward: Awkward means difficult to deal with, clumsy or uncomfortable.
bargain: A bargain is something bought for less than the usual price, or an agreement between people.
bruise: A bruise is a dark mark on skin caused by a bump or injury.
category: A category is a group of things that share the same type or feature.
cemetery: A cemetery is a place where people are buried.
committee: A committee is a group of people chosen to discuss or organise something.
communicate: To communicate means to share information, ideas or feelings.
community: A community is a group of people who live near each other or share something in common.
competition: A competition is an event where people try to win or do better than others.
conscience: Your conscience is the inner sense that helps you know right from wrong.
conscious: Conscious means awake, aware or able to notice what is happening.
controversy: A controversy is strong disagreement about an issue.
convenience: Convenience means ease, usefulness or something that saves time and effort.
correspond: To correspond means to match, agree or communicate by messages.
criticise: To criticise means to say what is wrong with something or someone.
curiosity: Curiosity is the wish to know, learn or find out about something.
definite: Definite means clear, certain and not vague.
desperate: Desperate means feeling that you urgently need something or have little hope.
determined: Determined means firmly decided to do something and not give up.
develop: To develop means to grow, improve or become more advanced.
dictionary: A dictionary is a book or resource that explains words, spellings and meanings.
disastrous: Disastrous means causing great harm, failure or trouble.
embarrass: To embarrass someone means to make them feel awkward, ashamed or self-conscious.
environment: The environment is the natural world around us, or the conditions in a place.
equip: To equip means to provide the things needed for a task.
equipped: Equipped means provided with what is needed.
equipment: Equipment is the things needed for a particular job or activity.
especially: Especially means particularly, or more than usual.
exaggerate: To exaggerate means to describe something as bigger, better or worse than it really is.
existence: Existence is the state of being real or alive.
explanation: An explanation gives reasons or details that make something clear.
familiar: Familiar means known to you because you have seen or experienced it before.
foreign: Foreign means from another country or not familiar.
forty: Forty is the number after thirty-nine and before forty-one.
frequently: Frequently means often or many times.
government: A government is the group of people who run a country or area.
guarantee: A guarantee is a firm promise that something will happen or work as expected.
harass: To harass means to repeatedly trouble, bother or upset someone.
hindrance: A hindrance is something that gets in the way or makes progress harder.
identity: Identity is who a person is, including the qualities that make them unique.
immediate: Immediate means happening now or without delay.
immediately: Immediately means straight away or without delay.
individual: An individual is one person or thing, separate from a group.
interfere: To interfere means to get involved in a way that causes problems or is not wanted.
interrupt: To interrupt means to stop someone while they are speaking or doing something.
language: Language is a system of words and signs that people use to communicate.
leisure: Leisure is free time when you can rest or enjoy activities.
lightning: Lightning is a bright flash of electricity in the sky during a storm.
marvellous: Marvellous means wonderful or very impressive.
mischievous: Mischievous means playful in a naughty or troublesome way.
muscle: A muscle is body tissue that tightens and relaxes to help you move.
neighbour: A neighbour is someone who lives near you.
nuisance: A nuisance is a person or thing that annoys or causes trouble.
occupy: To occupy means to take up space, live in a place or keep someone busy.
occur: To occur means to happen or take place.
opportunity: An opportunity is a chance to do something.
parliament: Parliament is the group of elected people who make laws in the UK and some other countries.
persuade: To persuade means to convince someone to do or believe something.
physical: Physical means connected with the body or with things you can touch.
prejudice: Prejudice is an unfair opinion about a person or group formed without enough knowledge.
privilege: A privilege is a special right or advantage.
profession: A profession is a type of work that needs special training or skill.
programme: A programme is a planned set of activities, or a television or radio show.
pronunciation: Pronunciation is the way a word is spoken.
queue: A queue is a line of people or things waiting for their turn.
recognise: To recognise means to know someone or something because you have seen it before.
recommend: To recommend means to say that someone should choose or try something.
relevant: Relevant means connected to what is being discussed or needed.
restaurant: A restaurant is a place where people pay to sit and eat meals.
rhyme: A rhyme is a word or line that has the same ending sound as another.
rhythm: Rhythm is a regular pattern of sounds, beats or movements.
sacrifice: A sacrifice is giving up something important for a reason or for someone else.
secretary: A secretary is a person who organises information, messages and office work.
shoulder: A shoulder is the part of the body where the arm joins the upper body.
signature: A signature is your name written in your own way to show agreement or identity.
sincere: Sincere means honest and truly meant.
soldier: A soldier is a person who serves in an army.
stomach: The stomach is the part inside your body where food begins to be digested.
sufficient: Sufficient means enough for what is needed.
suggest: To suggest means to put forward an idea or plan.
symbol: A symbol is a sign, shape or object that stands for an idea or meaning.
system: A system is a set of connected parts that work together.
temperature: Temperature tells how hot or cold something is.
thorough: Thorough means complete and careful, with nothing important missed.
twelfth: Twelfth means number twelve in order.
variety: Variety means a number of different kinds or a mixture.
vegetable: A vegetable is a plant or part of a plant eaten as food, such as a carrot.
vehicle: A vehicle is something used to carry people or goods, such as a car, bus or lorry.
yacht: A yacht is a boat used for sailing or pleasure trips.
